notes: 5 Anagrams + 5 Before & After. Rotating away from Homophones (used in wordplay-2026-06-20.json) and Homophones+B&A (used in wordplay-2026-06-19.json). Anagram clues follow edit-lessons §6: natural sentence whose MEANING the answer completes; scramble word sits naturally in sentence; NO fill-in-blank; NEVER call out the word. B&A clues describe both halves without naming either and without announcing the mechanic. All 10 answers are fresh — none appear in wordplay-001.json, wordplay-2026-06-17.json, wordplay-2026-06-18.json, wordplay-2026-06-19b.json, or wordplay-2026-06-20.json. Letter checks: brush=SHRUB ✓, snore=NORSE ✓, lures=RULES ✓, plums=SLUMP ✓, trail=TRIAL ✓.

questions.constraint_text: Rearrange the letters of one word in this clue to find the answer.
questions.clue: After breakfast, she reached for the brush and headed outside, where morning light made the little flowering plant beside the mailbox look almost golden.

questions.constraint_text: Rearrange the letters of one word in this clue to find the answer.
questions.clue: The scholar's snore echoed through the library all night, but come morning he had filled three notebooks with translations of the Viking-era manuscripts.

questions.clue: The fishing lures arranged in his tackle box were more like trophies than tools, and the strict regulations governing catch limits only added to the sport's appeal.

questions.clue: The kitchen was filled with plums, but despite the abundance of sweet fruit, the baker was in a creative rut she couldn't shake all week.

questions.clue: The hiking trail wound up the mountain, and at the summit twelve good citizens sat in quiet judgment of all they had witnessed below.
